title: Spring Controller Classes Should Not Use @Scope
description: Spring @Controllers, @Services, and @Repositorys have singleton scope by default. Defining any other scope for one of these class types will result in needless churn as new instances are created and destroyed. This rule raises an issue when the @Scope annotation is applied to a @Controller, @Service, or @Repository with any value but "singleton".
recommendation: Remove the @Scope annotation from @Controller, @Service, or @Repository classes.
impact: Prevent needless churn and additional load on the server.

checks.message: Remove the @Scope annotation from @Controller, @Service, or @Repository classes.
